title: NSX-T Segment and Gateway Capacity
exclusions: **Distributed firewall** rule economics, **DFW** statistics, or **micro-segmentation** coverage (**UC-18.2.x**); **Tier-0/Tier-1 active-standby SR failover** timelines and **HA** role swaps (**UC-18.2.12**); **Transport-node** **GENEVE** tunnel loss or **overlay path** degradation without **capacity** numerators (**UC-18.2.9**, **UC-18.2.5**); **BGP** **EVPN** **MAC** mobility storms on bare-metal **VTEP** fabrics (**UC-18.3.5**); **Cisco ACI** **MO** faults (**UC-18.1.x**). This UC stays on **overlay inventory**, **Manager capacity APIs**, **gateway** table headroom, and **Edge** dataplane ceilings.
dataSources: `index=vmware` / `index=vmware_nsx` / `index=network_ops` / `index=network_sec` (rename per governance) combining **five** core **`sourcetype`** lanes: **`vmware:nsxt:capacity`** (**`GET /api/v1/capacity/usage`** and **`GET /api/v1/capacity/threshold`** JSON mapped to **`capacity_key`**, **`current_usage`**, **`maximum`**, optional **`severity`**); **`vmware:nsxt:inventory`** (paged **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/segments`** aggregates such as **`segment_count`**, **`transport_zone_path`**); **`vmware:nsxt:gateway_status`** (**Tier-0/Tier-1** **Policy** posture plus route tallies when exposed); **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** (**Manager** capacity, threshold, maintenance narratives); **`vmware:nsxt:edge_ha`** (structured **Edge** health with dataplane/session cues ingestible via **TA** or **CLI** scrapers). Ingested through **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) with optional **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** from **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) for **Edge** VM labels only.
app: **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)); optional **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)); scripted **HTTPS**/**HEC** collectors for **`/api/v1/capacity/*`** and **Policy segment** inventory beyond stock inputs as required.
spl:
`comment("UC-18.3.7 NSX capacity idx tune warn 80 crit 92")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops OR index=network_sec) earliest=-6h@m latest=now
(
  sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:capacity"
  OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:gateway_status"
  OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:inventory"
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(capacity|segment|route.?table|arp|scale)"))
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_ha" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(dataplane|session.?table)"))
)
| eval cap_key=lower(trim(coalesce(capacity_key,resource_type,component,feature_name,"")))
| eval cap_curr=tonumber(coalesce(current_usage,used,current_value,count_observed,""))
| eval cap_max=tonumber(coalesce(maximum,max_value,limit_value,hard_limit,""))
| eval pct_use=if(cap_max>0 AND isnotnull(cap_curr), round(100*cap_curr/cap_max,2), null())
| eval tz_key=trim(coalesce(transport_zone_id,tz_uuid,zone_id,""))
| eval seg_key=trim(coalesce(segment_id,segment_path,ls_id,""))
| eval gw_key=trim(coalesce(gateway_path,tier0_path,tier1_path,lr_path,""))
| eval tier_hint=case(match(lower(gw_key),"(?i)tier_?0|t0"),"T0", match(lower(gw_key),"(?i)tier_?1|t1"),"T1", 1=1,"NA")
| eval route_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(route_count,rib_routes,fib_routes,bgp_route_count,""))
| eval seg_tz_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(segments_in_tz,segment_count,ls_per_tz,""))
| eval arp_fill=tonumber(coalesce(arp_entries,arp_table_size,neighbor_count,""))
| eval sess_used=tonumber(coalesce(session_table_used,sessions_in_use,""))
| eval sess_max=tonumber(coalesce(session_table_max,session_capacity,""))
| eval sess_pct=if(sess_max>0 AND isnotnull(sess_used), round(100*sess_used/sess_max,2), null())
| eval breach=case(
    pct_use>=92,"crit_usage_pct", pct_use>=80,"warn_usage_pct",
    sess_pct>=92,"crit_edge_session_pct", sess_pct>=80,"warn_edge_session_pct",
    tier_hint=="T0" AND route_cnt>=4500,"warn_t0_route_scale",
    len(tz_key)>0 AND seg_tz_cnt>=8500,"warn_tz_segment_count",
    arp_fill>=3600,"warn_arp_table", null())
| where isnotnull(breach)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats max(pct_use) AS pct_peak latest(cap_curr) AS usage_obs latest(cap_max) AS limit_obs
    max(sess_pct) AS sess_peak latest(route_cnt) AS routes_obs latest(seg_tz_cnt) AS segs_obs latest(arp_fill) AS arp_obs
    values(sourcetype) AS lanes values(cap_key) AS cap_keys dc(host) AS hops latest(tier_hint) AS tier
    BY _time breach gw_key tz_key seg_key
| eval story=case(
    breach=="crit_usage_pct","manager_capacity_critical",
    breach=="warn_usage_pct","manager_capacity_warning",
    breach=="crit_edge_session_pct","edge_session_critical",
    breach=="warn_edge_session_pct","edge_session_warning",
    breach=="warn_t0_route_scale","tier0_route_headroom_low",
    breach=="warn_tz_segment_count","transport_zone_segment_pressure",
    breach=="warn_arp_table","segment_neighbor_pressure",
    breach)
| sort 0 - _time - pct_peak - routes_obs
| head 500
| table _time story breach gw_key tz_key seg_key pct_peak usage_obs limit_obs sess_peak routes_obs segs_obs arp_obs tier lanes cap_keys hops
description: Monitors **VMware NSX** overlay **capacity** separate from firewall or failover storylines: **Manager** **`/api/v1/capacity/*`** usage versus thresholds, logical **segment** counts per **transport zone** near **~10k** planning decks, **Tier-0** aggregated route tallies approaching **~5k** workbench limits before expansion, **Tier-1** growth mirrored through **gateway_status** posture, **ARP**/neighbor occupancy per **segment** near **~4k** ceilings, and **Edge** dataplane **session** or memory pressure inferred from REST, **syslog**, **HA** feeds, or **`get dataplane`** companions. Splunk rolls the **five** **`vmware:nsxt:*`** sourcetypes into **`breach`** codes (**`manager_capacity_critical`**, **`tier0_route_headroom_low`**, **`transport_zone_segment_pressure`**, **`segment_neighbor_pressure`**, **`edge_session_critical`**) so operators see **scale risk** while **Policy** UIs still feel healthy.
value: Fabrics die quietly long before packets drop: **tenants** queue up **segments** and **routes** faster than **capital** budgets refresh **Edges**, and **session** tables exhaust during **holiday** spikes without a crisp early warning. Splunk gives **capacity**, **finance**, and **platform** teams shared evidence—when to order **larger** **Edges**, when to split **transport zones**, when to **pre-approve** **BGP** scale extensions—so **Sev-1** bridge calls trace to **decisions** made weeks earlier, not surprise **NAT** starvation during **IPO** week or **cloud** migrations.
implementation: Enable **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** inputs for **`vmware:nsxt:capacity`**, **`vmware:nsxt:inventory`**, **`vmware:nsxt:gateway_status`**, **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`**, and **`vmware:nsxt:edge_ha`**; script **`GET /api/v1/capacity/usage`**/**threshold** if absent; normalize **`FIELDALIAS`** for **gateway_path**/**transport_zone** keys; schedule **`nsxt_overlay_capacity_core`**; maintain **`macros`** and **`lookups`** for onboarding suppressions; optionally join **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** via **1810** for **Edge** VM **CMDB** context.
visualization: (1) **Single-value** — count of **`manager_capacity_critical`** vs **`edge_session_critical`** **24h**. (2) **Timechart** — **`pct_peak`** by **`story`**. (3) **Table** — **`gw_key`**, **`tz_key`**, **`routes_obs`**, **`segs_obs`**, **`arp_obs`**, **`sess_peak`**, **`lanes`**. (4) **Histogram** — **`segment_count`** distribution per **transport zone** for **planner** reviews. (5) **Map** (optional) — **site** metadata when **`lookup`** adds **data center** codes.
schema: **CIM `Performance`** frames numeric **utilization** ratios (**`pct_peak`**, **`sess_peak`**) tied to **entities** such as **edges** and **transport zones**. **`Inventory`** covers logical **segments**, **gateway paths**, and **route counts** harvested from **Policy** mirrors—map **dest** fields carefully because **NSX** reuse names differ from classic **Network_Traffic** flows.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **`Inventory`**-style summaries only after **`segment_path`** dedup stabilizes; keep raw **`vmware:nsxt:capacity`** unaccelerated until field schemas freeze post-upgrade.
knownFalsePositives:
• **greenfield_onboarding_spike** — Approved **tenant** waves deliberately load thousands of **segments** in one **change** weekend; Splunk **`transport_zone_segment_pressure`** positives should attach to lookup nsx_tz_onboarding_suppress.csv keyed on **`transport_zone_id`** when the **`CHG`** window is active, and otherwise align with **NSX** **onboarding** runbooks rather than silent drift.

• **api_page_drift_double_count** — Collector bugs replay the same **segment** **UUID** across pages; **`segs_obs`** overshoots **UI** totals until **`dedup segment_path`** ships—validate **`dc(segment_path)`** equals **Manager** **inventory** before paging **NetOps**.

• **capacity_counter_reset_upgrade** — **NSX** **Maintenance** releases reset **`usage_percent`** baselines; **`manager_capacity_warning`** spikes right after **boot** banners in **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`**—suppress **12h** when **`nsx_upgrade_suppress.csv`** lists the **cluster** **build**.

• **arp_learning_burst** — **ARP** **suppression** toggles or **VM** **anti-spoof** work triggers mass relearn resembling table pressure—correlate with **`vmware:nsxt:audit`** **segment** edits before **neighbor** escalations.

• **route_reflectors_external_routes** — **Tier-0** carries **WAN** **learned** **routes** that legitimately approach **scale** limits during **carrier** **turnups**—pair **`tier0_route_headroom_low`** with **carrier** **`CHG`** and **BGP** **advertisement** plans.

• **edge_form_factor_reprofile** — **Small**-to-**Large** **Edge** swaps raise **`session_capacity`** overnight; **`edge_session_warning`** clears after **`edge_form_factor.csv`** refresh—treat as **data** hygiene, not attack.

• **stale_rest_vs_datapath_truth** — **`get dataplane`** shows healthy **session** tables while **REST** omits **`sess_max`**; **`sess_pct`** stays **NULL** and **`warn_edge_session_pct`** never fires—do not declare green from **search** alone without **CLI** spot checks.
hardware: **VMware NSX** **4.2** / **4.1** / **NSX-T 3.2.x** **Manager** clusters (VIP-backed), **NSX Edge** nodes (**Small** through **XL** profiles), and **ESXi** **transport nodes** hosting overlay **TN** services sized per **Broadcom** reference designs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) on **Heavy Forwarders** or **Search Heads** that parse NSX feeds for VMware NSX 4.2, 4.1, and NSX-T 3.2.x trains per Broadcom lifecycle pages. Add **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) only when **vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter** joins must decorate Edge VM or transport-node names for leadership dashboards; optional, not a substitute for 4856 ingest.
• **RBAC and network** — Use a least-privilege operator that can **GET /api/v1/capacity/usage**, **GET /api/v1/capacity/threshold**, and paged **GET /policy/api/v1/infra/segments** without **DELETE verbs**. From Splunk, validate TCP 443 to the **NSX Manager VIP** or **NSX Policy** cluster, corporate proxy bypass rules, and credential rotation through **passwords.conf** references only.
• **Scale cheat-sheet pinned internally** — Document NSX 4.2 planning decks beside this UC: about **10k** logical segments per transport zone, **200** **Tier-0** gateways, **6k** **Tier-1** gateways, **~5k** **Tier-0** routes before expansion workflows, **~4k** ARP entries per segment, and Edge session or forwarding table ceilings that vary by Small/Medium/Large/XL form factor. Thresholds in the SPL are planning guards, not vendor alarms.
• **Scope guardrails** — Overlay capacity only: counts, table sizes, Manager usage endpoints, Edge dataplane pressure. Not distributed firewall economics (**UC-18.2.x**), SR failover timelines (**UC-18.2.12**), or pure **underlay packet loss**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• **vmware:nsxt:capacity** — Scripted HTTPS collectors (**Python modular input**, Lambda, or **cron + curl**) authenticate via **POST /api/session/create** or **API tokens**, then poll **GET /api/v1/capacity/usage** plus **GET /api/v1/capacity/threshold** on **10–30 minute** cadences with jitter. Flatten **capacity_key**, **current_usage**, maximum, optional severity, derived **usage_percent**, and _time aligned to **Manager clock**. Expect 10–50 KB per poll when every feature row ships.
• **vmware:nsxt:inventory** — Page **GET /policy/api/v1/infra/segments**; emit aggregates **segment_count**, **transport_zone_path**, vlan_id, **unique_gateway_paths** rather than every UUID each cycle when feasible. Use ETag or revision fields to skip unchanged payloads.
• **vmware:nsxt:gateway_status** — Capture **Tier-0/Tier-1** posture and any published route tallies from **Policy JSON** (tier-0s, tier-1s, locale-services) on **5–15 minute** cadence so **gateway_path** keys remain joinable to **capacity rows**.
• **vmware:nsxt:syslog** — Forward **Manager syslog** (not only Edge) so capacity threshold traps, **disk pressure**, and **upgrade banners** explain gaps in **REST feeds**.
• **vmware:nsxt:edge_ha** — Keep HA JSON or **syslog lines** referencing **dataplane health**; optional Edge CLI **get dataplane** exporters can enrich **session_table_used**/**session_table_max** through HEC with explicit **edge_node_id** fields.
• **Optional interface stats** — Poll **GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{tn-id}/node/interfaces/stats** when you must correlate physical NIC pressure with overlay growth; keep that feed in a separate sourcetype unless macros merge it sparingly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Publish saved search **nsxt_overlay_capacity_core** on a 15-minute schedule. Move thresholds **80**, **92**, **4500**, **8500**, and **3600** into **macros/nsxt_capacity.conf** so governance teams retune without rewriting SPL in **production**.

```spl
`comment("UC-18.3.7 NSX capacity idx tune warn 80 crit 92")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops OR index=network_sec) earliest=-6h@m latest=now
(
  sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:capacity"
  OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:gateway_status"
  OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:inventory"
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(capacity|segment|route.?table|arp|scale)"))
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_ha" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(dataplane|session.?table)"))
)
| eval cap_key=lower(trim(coalesce(capacity_key,resource_type,component,feature_name,"")))
| eval cap_curr=tonumber(coalesce(current_usage,used,current_value,count_observed,""))
| eval cap_max=tonumber(coalesce(maximum,max_value,limit_value,hard_limit,""))
| eval pct_use=if(cap_max>0 AND isnotnull(cap_curr), round(100*cap_curr/cap_max,2), null())
| eval tz_key=trim(coalesce(transport_zone_id,tz_uuid,zone_id,""))
| eval seg_key=trim(coalesce(segment_id,segment_path,ls_id,""))
| eval gw_key=trim(coalesce(gateway_path,tier0_path,tier1_path,lr_path,""))
| eval tier_hint=case(match(lower(gw_key),"(?i)tier_?0|t0"),"T0", match(lower(gw_key),"(?i)tier_?1|t1"),"T1", 1=1,"NA")
| eval route_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(route_count,rib_routes,fib_routes,bgp_route_count,""))
| eval seg_tz_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(segments_in_tz,segment_count,ls_per_tz,""))
| eval arp_fill=tonumber(coalesce(arp_entries,arp_table_size,neighbor_count,""))
| eval sess_used=tonumber(coalesce(session_table_used,sessions_in_use,""))
| eval sess_max=tonumber(coalesce(session_table_max,session_capacity,""))
| eval sess_pct=if(sess_max>0 AND isnotnull(sess_used), round(100*sess_used/sess_max,2), null())
| eval breach=case(
    pct_use>=92,"crit_usage_pct", pct_use>=80,"warn_usage_pct",
    sess_pct>=92,"crit_edge_session_pct", sess_pct>=80,"warn_edge_session_pct",
    tier_hint=="T0" AND route_cnt>=4500,"warn_t0_route_scale",
    len(tz_key)>0 AND seg_tz_cnt>=8500,"warn_tz_segment_count",
    arp_fill>=3600,"warn_arp_table", null())
| where isnotnull(breach)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats max(pct_use) AS pct_peak latest(cap_curr) AS usage_obs latest(cap_max) AS limit_obs
    max(sess_pct) AS sess_peak latest(route_cnt) AS routes_obs latest(seg_tz_cnt) AS segs_obs latest(arp_fill) AS arp_obs
    values(sourcetype) AS lanes values(cap_key) AS cap_keys dc(host) AS hops latest(tier_hint) AS tier
    BY _time breach gw_key tz_key seg_key
| eval story=case(
    breach=="crit_usage_pct","manager_capacity_critical",
    breach=="warn_usage_pct","manager_capacity_warning",
    breach=="crit_edge_session_pct","edge_session_critical",
    breach=="warn_edge_session_pct","edge_session_warning",
    breach=="warn_t0_route_scale","tier0_route_headroom_low",
    breach=="warn_tz_segment_count","transport_zone_segment_pressure",
    breach=="warn_arp_table","segment_neighbor_pressure",
    breach)
| sort 0 - _time - pct_peak - routes_obs
| head 500
| table _time story breach gw_key tz_key seg_key pct_peak usage_obs limit_obs sess_peak routes_obs segs_obs arp_obs tier lanes cap_keys hops
```

Understanding this SPL — pct_use lights when Manager publishes **numerator/denominator** pairs in **vmware:nsxt:capacity**. route_cnt, seg_tz_cnt, and arp_fill catch planning limits when REST usage lags **inventory truth**. sess_pct needs Edge exporters with honest session_table_max; otherwise **crit_edge_session_pct** stays quiet by design—do not treat NULL **session math** as healthy without CLI checks. breach collapses multiple physics into **story labels** for ticketing; lanes lists sourcetype lanes so silent feeds are obvious during reviews.

Step 3 — Validate
• Compare segs_obs deltas with NSX Manager UI **Networking ▸ Segments** filtered per transport zone; Splunk should trail by at most two **poll intervals**.
• Compare pct_peak against System ▸ Capacity dashboards for the same capacity_key; mismatches imply duplicate collectors or timezone skew on forwarders.
• For **tier0_route_headroom_low**, spot-check routes_obs against Tier-0 routing status screens or SR CLI—4500 is a governance threshold tied to NSX 4.2 planning guidance, not a guaranteed hard stop.
• Run **timechart span=15m** count by sourcetype; a flat vmware:nsxt:capacity line while UI climbs signals **HTTP 401/403**, **VIP mis-affinity**, or **pagination truncation**.
• When **edge_session_warning** appears, SSH (per change policy) to the Edge and run get dataplane to confirm session tables track sess_peak.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Place single-value tiles for **manager_capacity_critical** and **edge_session_critical**, a 15-minute pct_peak timechart stacked by story, and a sortable table with gw_key, tz_key, routes_obs, segs_obs, arp_obs, lanes for NOC review. Route alerts on story with throttles per gw_key plus tz_key tuple. Join **planned_change lookups** to suppress transport_zone_segment_pressure during approved onboarding waves. Archive weekly CSV extracts for **finance governance** and mirror saved searches to the **disaster-recovery** cluster so **NSX Manager failover** does not strand analytics.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
**Dashboard layout** — On NSX_Overlay_Capacity_Exec, stack three rows for **segment pressure**, **gateway capacity**, and **session utilization**: top row uses a **timechart** of pct_peak by story with a sortable table of hot segs_obs per transport zone; middle row uses **single-value** or **gauge** tiles for routes_obs alongside tier0_route_headroom_low and large ARP rows; bottom row uses **gauge** or **single-value** for sess_peak and sess_pct by edge_node_id when session_table_max is present. Add a slim ingest **timechart** by sourcetype so a flat vmware:nsxt:capacity feed is visible before KPIs drift.
**Alert design and notification rules** — Page **manager_capacity_critical** and **edge_session_critical** within fifteen minutes; ticket **transport_zone_segment_pressure** and **tier0_route_headroom_low** with planned_change lookups; send daily digest email only for warn-prefixed breach codes. Throttle fifteen minutes per gw_key + tz_key + breach unless severity escalates. Suppress with nsx_tz_onboarding_suppress.csv and nsx_upgrade_suppress.csv during approved windows. Webhook or email payloads should carry pct_peak, routes_obs, segs_obs, sess_peak, arp_obs, and lanes for triage.
• **No vmware:nsxt:capacity events** — Inspect modular input logs for HTTP 429/503, expired tokens, or certificate trust failures; replicate curl from the same host Splunk uses.
• **Duplicate capacity_key spellings** — Different collectors may emit Segment count versus SEGMENT_COUNT; normalize with EVAL and FIELDALIAS stanzas in props.conf before declaring UI drift.
• **routes_obs always NULL** — **Policy feeds** might omit RIB size; augment vmware:nsxt:gateway_status parsing or accept that Tier-0 pressure must be validated manually until those fields exist.
• **segs_obs inflation** — Missing dedup on segment_path during **inventory harvest** double-counts; compare dc(segment_path) to UI totals before paging NetOps.
• **False session pressure** — session_table_max reads zero when collectors omit it; ignore sess_pct until sess_max is greater than zero.

Governance — Document macro owners, link internal scale tables to **Broadcom Configuration Maximums** for NSX 4.2, require two approvers for SPL changes that touch this UC, and rehearse onboarding suppressions quarterly so transport_zone_segment_pressure stays trustworthy.
grandmaExplanation: We help you notice when the virtual network is nearly full of new subnets, gateway route tables, and edge connection tracking, well before fresh tenants hit slowdowns or failures.

controlTest.positiveScenario: **vmware:nsxt:capacity** events report **LOGICAL_SWITCH** usage near ninety-four percent while **vmware:nsxt:inventory** counts exceed eight thousand nine hundred segments for the same **transport_zone**; Splunk emits **manager_capacity_critical** with no matching maintenance ticket.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During an **NSX** cluster upgrade, **vmware:nsxt:syslog** documents maintenance banners and **vmware:nsxt:capacity** usage drops briefly cluster-wide; **nsx_upgrade_suppress.csv** lists the active **build** and the alert stays quiet for the twelve-hour suppress window documented in the runbook.

evidence: **Saved search** `nsxt_overlay_capacity_core` scheduled every fifteen minutes plus dashboard **NSX_Overlay_Capacity_Exec**; weekly PDF or CSV exports archived to **index=sre_governance** for capacity governance and external audit requests.
